Comfort.definition: a state of being relaxed and feeling no pain

Reassure.definition: cause to feel sure; give reassurance to

Soothe.definition: give moral or emotional strength to

Support.definition: give moral or psychological support, aid, or courage to

Care.definition: the work of providing treatment for or attending to someone or something

Cuddle.definition: a close and affectionate (and often prolonged) embrace

Pat.definition: pat or squeeze fondly or playfully, especially under the chin

Caress.definition: a gentle affectionate stroking (or something resembling it)

Stroke.definition: a light touch with the hands

Hold.definition: have or possess, either in a concrete or an abstract sense

Cheer.definition: a cry or shout of approval

Encourage.definition: inspire with confidence; give hope or courage to

Grounding.definition: confine or restrict to the ground

Sad.definition: experiencing or showing sorrow or unhappiness; ; - Christina Rossetti

Upset.definition: an unhappy and worried mental state
the act of disturbing the mind or body

Cry.definition: a loud utterance; often in protest or opposition

Anxiety.definition: (psychiatry) a relatively permanent state of worry and nervousness occurring in a variety of mental disorders, usually accompanied by compulsive behavior or attacks of panic
a vague unpleasant emotion that is experienced in anticipation of some (usually ill-defined) misfortune

Anxious.definition: causing or fraught with or showing anxiety

Stress.definition: (psychology) a state of mental or emotional strain or suspense

Worried.definition: be worried, concerned, anxious, troubled, or uneasy

Emotional.definition: determined or actuated by emotion rather than reason
